description: A set of instructions for preparing a particular food or drink. Recipes live in speech, memory, demonstration, and writing, and some leave quantities to practiced judgment. 'norae' (method) is a way of doing any activity; 'thekopa' ties the method to ingredients and a prepared result.
sound_symbolism: Fine dental breath opens 'the' into balanced 'e'. Stressed 'ko' closes at the velum and rounds the middle. Final 'pa' moves the closure to the lips and opens through broad 'a'. Breath and two firm contacts lead to the word's widest vowel.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia ha thekopa theo.' (I read this recipe.) Instructions, ingredients, sequence, and intended result can be described in Phi. Exact quantities, temperatures, timings, and source wording stay with the source recipe when their notation matters.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Many recipes lived in hands and memory, with the written line arriving later than the smell, texture, and timing it tried to preserve.

semantic_domains.cognition: remembered or recorded knowledge for preparing food
semantic_domains.creation: instructions relating ingredients to a prepared result
semantic_domains.temporal: an ordered sequence of preparation steps
